title: struthiopteris fraseri
intro: Struthiopteris fraseri, also known as the Fraser's leather fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. It is native to the eastern parts of North America and is often found in shaded, humid areas. The medicinal properties of Struthiopteris fraseri include its ability to act as a demulcent and astringent, making it effective in treating skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves for topical application.
